tasks.title: Initialize repository structure
acceptance_criteria: Directory tree matches spec exactly: prompts/, docs/, src/, tests/ all exist
src/__init__.py and tests/__init__.py are present (empty or minimal)
README.md exists with project title and one-line description
prompts/architect.md, prompts/engineer.md, prompts/shared_rules.md exist as placeholders
docs/protocol.md and docs/workflow.md exist as placeholders
src/handoff_parser.py, src/task_store.py, src/models.py exist as placeholder stubs
tests/test_handoff_parser.py and tests/test_task_state.py exist as placeholder stubs
No production logic implemented — placeholders only
All files are syntactically valid Python (import-safe stubs) or valid Markdown

tasks.title: Define workflow models and enums
acceptance_criteria: TaskStatus enum defined with values: planned, in_engineering, review, approved, rework, complete
AgentRole enum defined with values: architect, engineer, human
Task dataclass defined with fields: id, title, status, acceptance_criteria, constraints, files_to_touch, changed_files, validation, blockers
HandoffMessage dataclass defined with all handoff block fields plus timestamp
ValidationResult dataclass defined with: valid (bool), errors (list[str])
All models importable from src.models
Models include __repr__ and equality support via dataclass defaults

tasks.title: Implement handoff parser
acceptance_criteria: parse_handoff_file(path) reads a file and returns list[HandoffMessage]
get_latest_valid_block(path) returns the last valid HandoffMessage or None
validate_block(raw_text) returns a ValidationResult with errors for missing required fields
Malformed blocks (missing ---END--- or required fields) are rejected with descriptive errors
Parser is importable from src.handoff_parser

tasks.title: Implement task store
acceptance_criteria: TaskStore class implemented in src/task_store.py
load(path) reads tasks.json and populates internal store
save(path) persists current state to tasks.json
get(task_id) returns Task or raises KeyError
update_status(task_id, new_status) persists status change
list_tasks() returns all tasks
All operations are idempotent for same-status updates

tasks.title: Implement task lifecycle rules
acceptance_criteria: VALID_TRANSITIONS map defined: planned→in_engineering, in_engineering→review, review→approved, review→rework, rework→in_engineering, approved→complete
update_status raises ValueError for invalid transitions with message naming both states
At most one task may be in in_engineering state at a time; attempting a second raises ValueError
Lifecycle rules enforced inside TaskStore.update_status

tasks.title: Add workflow helper logic
acceptance_criteria: derive_next_actor(message: HandoffMessage) -> AgentRole implemented
is_completion_guard_met(tasks: list[Task]) -> bool returns True only when all tasks have status complete
needs_human_escalation(message: HandoffMessage) -> bool returns True when NEXT==human or STATUS==needs_human
Helpers importable from src.workflow or src.handoff_parser
Each helper has a docstring explaining its contract

tasks.title: Write tests
acceptance_criteria: test_handoff_parser.py covers: valid block parse, malformed block rejection, missing required field, multi-block file with mixed valid/invalid, get_latest_valid_block
test_task_state.py covers: all valid transition paths, invalid transition raises ValueError, in_engineering concurrency guard, load/save round-trip
An end-to-end test parses a handoff, updates task store, derives next actor
All tests pass with: python -m pytest tests/
No external test dependencies beyond pytest

tasks.title: Finalize documentation
acceptance_criteria: README.md contains: project title, description, setup instructions, usage examples, file layout
docs/protocol.md documents the full handoff block format with field definitions and allowed values
docs/workflow.md describes the agent workflow with phase descriptions and state transitions
prompts/architect.md contains the architect role prompt usable as a system prompt
prompts/engineer.md contains the engineer role prompt usable as a system prompt
prompts/shared_rules.md contains the session rules shared by both agents

tasks.title: Final review and completion
acceptance_criteria: Architect has reviewed all deliverables against their acceptance criteria
All tests pass
File layout matches spec exactly
handoff.md integrity check: all blocks are well-formed, append-only invariant holds
Architect appends STATUS: plan_complete block to declare completion

tasks.title: EventLog enhancements and turn observability (1.1, 1.2, 1.3)
acceptance_criteria: Every event in workflow_events.jsonl includes a response_text field containing the agent full output (empty string when output is empty, never omitted)
Existing event fields (ts, turn, agent, task_id, status_before, status_after, session_id) are unchanged
After a workflow run, prompts_log/ contains one .md file per turn named turn-NNN-agent.md (zero-padded to 3 digits)
Each prompt file contains the exact prompt string passed to the runner
The event record includes prompt_file field with the relative path (e.g. prompts_log/turn-001-architect.md)
The event record includes prompt_hash field with the SHA-256 hex digest of the prompt
Every event record includes duration_seconds as a float rounded to 2 decimal places
Duration is measured with time.monotonic() around the runner call only, not TUI or post-processing
Long response_text values are not truncated; newlines are escaped as \n by json.dumps
Each record remains a single valid JSON line in the JSONL file
All existing tests pass without modification

tasks.title: Log task state transition failures (1.4)
acceptance_criteria: All event records include a type field: turn for normal events, warning for warnings
When a task transition fails, workflow_events.jsonl contains a record with type: warning and the transition error message
The warning event includes turn, agent, task_id, and the attempted transition (from status to status)
The coordinator does not exit or crash on transition failures
Warning events use the same JSONL format as turn events

tasks.title: Enhanced workflow summary on completion (1.5)
acceptance_criteria: On workflow completion, the summary includes total turns and per-agent breakdown (existing behavior preserved)
Task summary shown: N done, N in-progress, N blocked (when tasks.json exists)
Total rework cycles across all tasks shown
Total wall-clock duration (first turn start to last turn end) shown
If tasks.json does not exist, only the turn summary is shown
The existing _print_summary() function signature is unchanged; new output is added after it

tasks.title: Display protocol abstraction (2.1)
acceptance_criteria: agent_coordinator/application/display.py exists with a Display Protocol class using typing.Protocol
Display protocol defines methods: start_run(), start_agent_turn(), update_output(), finish_agent_turn(), show_progress(), show_interrupt_menu(), close()
cli.py imports and type-hints against Display, not TUIDisplay
TUIDisplay satisfies the Display protocol without changes to its public API or class hierarchy
A PlainDisplay class exists for non-TTY/--quiet mode that implements Display with simple print() calls
No abstract base classes used — protocol is structural (typing.Protocol)
All existing tests pass without modification

tasks.title: Workflow progress in status bar (2.2)
acceptance_criteria: The turn header includes turn counter (e.g. Turn 7/30)
Task progress shown when tasks.json exists (e.g. Tasks: 3/7 done)
Rework count shown (e.g. Reworks: 1)
Progress updates after each turn completes
When task_service is None, only the turn counter is shown
show_progress() is implemented in both TUIDisplay and PlainDisplay

tasks.title: Enhanced interrupt menu with context (2.3)
acceptance_criteria: Pressing Ctrl+C shows a context block above the menu options
Context includes: current turn/max turns, active agent and backend, current task ID and status, elapsed time, task progress summary
If task_service is unavailable, task-related fields are omitted
Menu options remain unchanged: c, r, e, m, i, q
Context display uses the same color scheme as the existing TUI

tasks.title: Guided human input builder (2.4)
acceptance_criteria: A new option g (guided) exists in the human prompt menu
Guided flow prompts for STATUS (choice from valid values), NEXT (choice from agents + human + none), SUMMARY (free text)
ROLE is always human; TASK_ID and TITLE are carried forward from the previous block
The generated block is shown for review before being appended
The user can abort at any step without modifying handoff.md
The generated block passes HandoffParser validation
Existing r and e options remain available
Uses enhanced_input for readline support
Rejects logically invalid STATUS/NEXT combinations (e.g. approved + developer)

tasks.title: Dry-run mode (2.5)
acceptance_criteria: agent-coordinator --workspace ./project --dry-run prints the full prompt and exits with code 0
The prompt printed is identical to what would be sent to the runner
No files are modified (handoff.md, tasks.json, workflow_events.jsonl, sessions)
If the workflow would be terminal (plan_complete, blocked), print the stop reason instead
Dry-run always exits after printing — does not enter the loop
Output goes to stdout with no TUI formatting

tasks.title: Configurable prompt sections per agent (3.1)
acceptance_criteria: An agent with include_spec: false does not receive SPECIFICATION.md content in any turn
An agent with include_plan: false does not receive plan.md content in any turn
An agent with max_handoff_lines: 100 receives only the last 100 lines of handoff.md
An agent with extra_files: [API.md] receives the content of workspace/API.md as a section
Default behavior (no prompt key) is identical to current behavior
Prompt injection order is unchanged — only inclusion/exclusion is controlled
extra_files paths are relative to workspace root; missing files are skipped silently
max_handoff_lines truncates from the beginning, keeping most recent content
All existing tests pass without modification

tasks.title: Cycle detection (3.2)
acceptance_criteria: A workflow looping A→B→A→B→A→B (3 cycles same agent+task_id) triggers a warning
Warning includes the cycling agents and task ID
The interrupt menu is shown for user intervention
If the user continues, the cycle counter resets
Cycle detection does not trigger for legitimate review loops where task status changes between cycles
Status changes (continue → rework_required → continue) reset the cycle counter
A warning event is logged to workflow_events.jsonl

tasks.title: Per-agent turn timeout (3.3)
acceptance_criteria: A backend exceeding timeout_seconds is terminated
Event log records a timeout event with type: timeout and duration_seconds
Retry logic runs after a timeout (same as when handoff.md is not updated)
Default timeout of 300 seconds applies when no explicit config is set
Per-agent timeout_seconds overrides the global timeout
Manual runner is exempt from timeout
Uses subprocess.Popen communicate(timeout=N) for subprocess-based runners

tasks.title: Hot-reload agents.json (3.4)
acceptance_criteria: Editing agents.json during a workflow run takes effect on the next turn
Changing an agent backend mid-workflow creates a new runner (old sessions preserved)
Changing retry policy mid-workflow takes effect immediately
A notice is printed when config reload is detected
If agents.json has a syntax error, the previous valid config is retained and a warning is printed
Session IDs are not invalidated on config change
Config reload only happens at start of each turn, not during sub-operations

tasks.title: Workspace init command (4.1)
acceptance_criteria: agent-coordinator init --workspace ./my-project creates a valid workspace
Running agent-coordinator --workspace ./my-project immediately after init starts a working workflow
Existing files are never overwritten; a notice is printed for skipped files
--no-editor skips the editor step
Backend auto-detection: checks PATH for gh, claude, opencode and sets default_backend accordingly
Created agents.json uses the same schema as the existing default
Prompt files are copied from the installed package, not symlinked
Init does not start a workflow — only scaffolds files

tasks.title: History and analysis command (4.2)
acceptance_criteria: agent-coordinator history --workspace ./project prints a chronological timeline of turns
agent-coordinator history --workspace ./project --tasks prints per-task lifecycle summary with rework counts
agent-coordinator history --workspace ./project --stats prints aggregate metrics (duration, rework rate, per-agent breakdown)
Timeline mode works with existing event format
Tasks mode cross-references workflow_events.jsonl with tasks.json when available
Stats mode uses duration_seconds field; shows N/A if absent
Empty event log produces No events found message, not an error
Output is plain text to stdout (no TUI, no colors unless --color)
Never modifies any files (read-only)

tasks.title: Event hooks (4.3)
acceptance_criteria: A hooks/on-turn-complete script in the workspace is called after each turn
A hooks/on-task-done script is called when a task transitions to done
A hooks/on-workflow-complete script is called when the workflow finishes
Scripts receive event record as JSON on stdin
Scripts that do not exist are silently skipped
Scripts that fail (non-zero exit, timeout) produce a warning in the event log
Scripts that hang beyond 10 seconds are killed
No hooks/ directory means no overhead
Hooks receive WORKSPACE environment variable
Coordinator does not wait for hooks before next turn (async execution)

tasks.title: Final integration review and documentation
acceptance_criteria: All 188+ existing tests pass
All new tests pass
All 17 spec requirements have been verified against acceptance criteria
README.md updated with new CLI flags and features
Backward compatibility verified: existing workflows work unchanged
